title: Incident Room: Explain the Page Without Hand-Waving
summary: Reconstruct an unfamiliar page incident by labeling observed, inferred, and documented claims across all six foundation models.
objectives: Integrate system, file, network, rendering, tooling, and research responsibilities.
Separate direct observations from inferences and verified documentation.

steps.title: Interpret the trace · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, what does the read event establish?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through interpret the trace narrows the emergency-notice incident room while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Interpret the trace — emergency-notice incident room
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the emergency-notice incident room evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room answer, identify which system chain boundary “Interpret the trace” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Interpret the trace” evidence during answer.
For emergency-notice incident room answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Interpret the trace.”

options.text: The browser permanently rewrote the file as soon as the pointer was clicked.
The operating system supplied stored document bytes before browser parsing began.
The display requested HTML tags directly from storage.

steps.title: Assign request and response · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, which event belongs to the server side of the web exchange?
steps.why: Local-file access and web requests can display similar pages but fail at different boundaries. Working through assign request and response narrows the emergency-notice incident room while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Assign request and response — emergency-notice incident room
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the emergency-notice incident room evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room inspect, identify which load journey boundary “Assign request and response” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Assign request and response” evidence during inspect.
For emergency-notice incident room inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Assign request and response.”

options.text: Painting the received document into the learner’s browser window.
Returning an HTTP status, headers, and resource bytes to the browser.
Interpreting a pointer click on the learner’s input device.

stimulus.title: Two notice loads · emergency-notice incident room
stimulus.caption: Evidence from opening a saved file and visiting the published notice. Case: emergency-notice incident room; focus: assign request and response.

steps.title: Rebuild browser pipeline · incident room
steps.instruction: Within the emergency-notice incident room, reconstruct the browser pipeline in causal order.
steps.why: Source, the browser-built DOM, layout, and pixels are related stages rather than interchangeable evidence. Ordering the emergency-notice incident room exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the emergency-notice incident room.

hints: In the emergency-notice incident room sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate emergency-notice incident room browser pipeline events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the emergency-notice incident room sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Document bytes arrive as source input
The parser tokenizes source and applies recovery rules
The browser constructs the current document tree
Style, layout, and paint produce visible pixels

steps.title: Check letter case · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, source requests Images/Map.svg, while the tree contains images/map.svg. What is the durable repair?
steps.why: Portable paths preserve the relationship between project resources when the containing folder moves. Working through check letter case narrows the emergency-notice incident room before the result is shown.

content.title: Check letter case — emergency-notice incident room
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the emergency-notice incident room evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room predict, identify which project paths boundary “Check letter case” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Check letter case” evidence during predict.
For emergency-notice incident room predict, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Check letter case.”

options.text: Add spaces until both strings have the same character count.
Make the path casing exactly match the directory and filename casing.
Depend on all servers treating differently cased paths as identical.

steps.title: Choose the right panel · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, a stylesheet request returns 404. Which panel provides the direct evidence?
steps.why: A deliberate panel choice turns browser inspection into evidence instead of random experimentation. Working through choose the right panel narrows the emergency-notice incident room as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: Developer tools expose different evidence: Elements for the current DOM, Styles and Computed for declared and resolved CSS, Layout for geometry, Accessibility for names and roles, Network for requests and responses, and Sources for loaded code. Temporary panel edits test a hypothesis but do not save project files. Apply this distinction during the emergency-notice incident room read task.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room read, identify which devtools evidence boundary “Choose the right panel” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Choose the right panel” evidence during read.
For emergency-notice incident room read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Choose the right panel.”

options.text: Elements, because DOM nodes generate every HTTP status.
Network, filtered to the stylesheet request and response status.
The color picker, because missing resources are color defects.

steps.title: Check source scope · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, before applying the standard text, what must be confirmed?
steps.why: Technical guidance is safer when authority, currency, scope, and browser evidence agree. Working through check source scope narrows the emergency-notice incident room while you inspect the artifact.

content.title: Check source scope — emergency-notice incident room
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the emergency-notice incident room evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room inspect, identify which source verification boundary “Check source scope” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Check source scope” evidence during inspect.
For emergency-notice incident room inspect, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Check source scope.”

options.text: That the page uses the same brand colors as the project.
That the cited section addresses the same feature, conditions, and current version under investigation.
That the search engine placed it above all official documentation.

stimulus.title: Conflicting guidance · emergency-notice incident room
stimulus.caption: Three sources returned for the same browser-behavior question. Case: emergency-notice incident room; focus: check source scope.

lines.label: test
lines.text: reduced case reproduces in current browser

steps.title: Locate a failure · incident room
steps.instruction: In the emergency-notice incident room, the file bytes are read, but no browser process starts. Which boundary should be investigated next?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through locate a failure narrows the emergency-notice incident room while you defend the selected claim.

content.title: Locate a failure — emergency-notice incident room
content.text: Commit to the claim that explains the emergency-notice incident room evidence without assigning work to the wrong layer.

hints: During emergency-notice incident room answer, identify which system chain boundary “Locate a failure” asks about.
Compare every emergency-notice incident room option with the named “Locate a failure” evidence during answer.
For emergency-notice incident room answer, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Locate a failure.”

options.text: The CSS color selected by the page author.
The operating-system handoff that launches or addresses the browser application.
The monitor brightness setting, because no parsing event exists yet.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · incident room
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the emergency-notice incident room. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the emergency-notice incident room from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the emergency-notice incident room case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these emergency-notice incident room technical anchors where they fit: operating system, browser, display, URL, request.
Close the emergency-notice incident room note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
